question: What is the primary purpose of a Product Requirements Document (PRD)?

choices.A: To define the budget and timeline for a project
choices.B: To describe what a product should do and why, so engineering and design can build it
choices.C: To document bugs found during QA testing
choices.D: To track sales performance of a launched product

question: Which framework is most useful for prioritising features when stakeholder requests exceed team capacity?
A North Star Metric is best described as:

choices.A: A metric that tracks monthly recurring revenue
choices.B: A single metric that captures the core value a product delivers to customers
choices.C: The number of daily active users divided by monthly active users
choices.D: A target set by the CEO for the fiscal year

question: In the context of product discovery, a 'jobs to be done' interview is designed to uncover:

choices.A: Which features users prefer in competitor products
choices.B: The functional and emotional progress users are trying to make in their lives
choices.C: How many hours per week users spend in the product
choices.D: Whether users are willing to pay for a premium tier

question: What distinguishes an output from an outcome in product strategy?
Briefly explain what an MVP (Minimum Viable Product) is and why teams build one.
In a typical Agile Scrum team, who is responsible for maximising the value of the product and managing the Product Backlog?
What is the purpose of a Sprint Retrospective?

choices.A: To plan the next sprint's backlog
choices.B: To demo completed work to stakeholders
choices.C: To inspect the past sprint and create a plan for improvements
choices.D: To estimate story points for upcoming features

question: In Agile, what is the difference between a user story and an acceptance criterion?
Which Agile principle best describes 'delivering working software frequently'?

choices.A: Principle 1: Customer satisfaction through early delivery
choices.B: Principle 3: Deliver working software frequently, from a couple of weeks to a couple of months
choices.C: Principle 6: Face-to-face conversation is the most efficient communication
choices.D: Principle 12: Reflect and adjust at regular intervals

question: What is 'velocity' in the context of a Scrum team?
Which technique is used to estimate work in Agile when the team has low certainty?
Explain what 'technical debt' means and give one reason a product team should care about it.
If a team has 5 members and each member needs to be able to communicate with every other member, how many unique communication channels are there?
A product has 1,000 daily active users. 100 users churn per month and 150 new users join per month. After three months, approximately how many DAUs does the product have?
A PM has three tasks: A (2 hours, blocks B), B (1 hour, blocks C), C (3 hours). What is the minimum time to complete all three tasks?
Describe what 'second-order thinking' means and give a product example where ignoring it led to a bad outcome.
What is the Pareto Principle (80/20 rule) and how might a PM apply it to bug triage?
In decision-making, what is 'analysis paralysis' and what strategy can a PM use to overcome it?
Which logical fallacy describes the tendency to favour information that confirms pre-existing beliefs?
